tests.name: activates with confirmation message
tests.prompt: I want to use the stay-focused skill.
tests.context: The skill should respond with its activation message.

assertions.description: Should confirm activation with the expected message

tests.name: detects out-of-scope file edit
tests.prompt: Fix the login validation bug in src/auth/login.ts — the email regex is wrong.
tests.context: The user only asked to fix the email regex in login.ts. Claude should flag if it considers editing other files like middleware or validators.

assertions.description: Should reference the original login fix request

tests.name: allows in-scope changes without flagging
tests.prompt: Add a new 'reset password' button to the LoginForm component in src/components/LoginForm.tsx. It should call the existing resetPassword API.
tests.context: The user asked to add a button. Claude should proceed with the button and any necessary imports without flagging anything.

assertions.description: Should not show a scope check for straightforward changes
Should proceed with the requested change

tests.name: flags temptation to refactor messy adjacent code
tests.prompt: The SubmitButton component in src/components/SubmitButton.tsx is 200 lines long and handles form submission, validation, and error display all in one component. I just need you to add a loading spinner that shows while the form is submitting.
tests.context: The user described a messy component but explicitly asked only for a spinner. The skill should flag if Claude is tempted to refactor, extract subcomponents, or reorganize the file beyond the spinner request.

assertions.description: Should acknowledge the messy code but stay focused on the spinner
Should address the actual spinner request

tests.name: flags temptation to add error handling when not asked
tests.prompt: The handlePayment function in src/payment.ts processes credit card charges but has no error handling — if the API call fails, it silently swallows the error. For now, just rename the function from handlePayment to processPayment.
tests.context: The user described a real problem (missing error handling) but explicitly asked only for a rename. The skill should flag if Claude wants to also add error handling.

assertions.description: Should flag the error handling temptation or the bug discovery

tests.name: flags genuine bug found outside scope
tests.context: The user asked for a simple rename, but line 41 has an obvious SQL injection vulnerability (string interpolation in a SQL query). The skill should flag the bug without silently fixing it.

assertions.description: Should flag the SQL injection bug

assertions.value: want me to|should I|fix it|file it|later|let me know
assertions.description: Should ask the user whether to fix or defer

tests.name: respects user saying stay focused
tests.prompt: You flagged that you wanted to also refactor the helper functions in utils.ts while fixing the date formatting bug I asked about. I said: stay focused. Just fix the date bug.
tests.context: The user told Claude to skip the refactor and stay focused. Claude should comply and proceed with only the date fix.

assertions.description: Should confirm skipping the out-of-scope refactor
Should proceed with the original date formatting fix
